fieldGroups.description: Diagnosis evidence, coding-sensitive rows, and escalation flags.

fieldGroups.label: Medications, Allergies, And Special Treatments
fieldGroups.description: Medication safety, allergies, injectables, and O0110 special treatments.

fieldGroups.label: Functional And Therapy Status
fieldGroups.description: GG/M functional scoring, therapy need, discipline frequency, and goals.

fieldGroups.label: Symptom And Body System Review
fieldGroups.description: Body-system review sections that QA checks for consistency and completeness.

fieldGroups.label: Risk And Safety Assessments
fieldGroups.description: Fall, wound, hospitalization, and safety-related risk tools.

fieldGroups.label: Care Plan And Coordination
fieldGroups.description: Plan-of-care orders, patient summary, skilled interventions, and care plan content.

fieldGroups.label: Supplementals And History
fieldGroups.description: Past medical history, caregiver support, living situation, immunizations, and supplementals.

fieldGroups.label: Low Priority Admin Reference
fieldGroups.description: Identity, agency, physician, and demographic reference values that should not dominate QA.

textSpans.text: To the extent patient information contained in this fax is subject to 42 CFR Part 2, this regulation
Schedule Within: provider's discretion Note to Provider:
Patient is a 68yoM who presents today following left partial 5th ray amputation due to gangrene on 2/13/26.
The patient was instructed to continue WBAT in CAM boot/post op shoe.
The patient was advised to follow up in 2 week
All questions were addressed, and the patient verbalized understanding of the recovery
WOUND CARE REFERRAL Schedule Within: provider's discretion Note to Provider: please initiate wound
ICD-10: T81.31XD: Disruption of external operation (surgical) wound,
Z48.89: Encounter for other specified surgical aftercare
E11.52: Type 2 diabetes mellitus with diabetic peripheral angiopathy with gangrene
T81.31XD: Disruption of external operation (surgical) wound, not elsewhere classified, subsequent
T81.31XD: Disruption of external operation (surgical) wound
clean and dry and monitoring for any signs of infection such as redness, drainage, or increasing pain.
please initiate wound vac therapy on a non-healing diabetic foot
Postoperative wound dehiscence, initial encounter - Onset: 03/02/2026
the wound base exhibited healthy, bleeding, granular tissue.
Hemostasis was achieved with pressure to
the wound and a dressing was then applied consisting of wet to dry dressing.
the depth of the wound, I will prescribe negative pressure wound therapy (wound vac), a wound care
Wound care instructions were reinforced, including keeping the area
vac therapy on a non-healing diabetic foot wound and perform MWF
ambulates in normal shoegear today no surgical shoe as his last one broke.
is okay as long as he wears his CAM boot when he does have to ambulate.
Type 2 diabetes mellitus with diabetic peripheral angiopathy with gangrene, unspecified whether long term

Cellulitis of toe of left foot - Onset: 03/02/2026
Surgical & Procedure History not reviewed (last reviewed 03/02/2026)
Past Medical History not reviewed (last reviewed 03/02/2026)
Examination of the left lower extremity reveals lightly palpable DP/PT pulses.
Surgical site is clean and dry.
No erythema noted around the distal lateral aspect of
Incisions are approximated with dehiscence noted at the distal aspect which is now a full-thickness
Sharp, excisional debridement was performed today in the office.
Debridement was carried down to, and
including, the level of subcutaneous tissue.
All necrotic, fibrotic, and non-viable tissue was removed until
The surgical site was evaluated and is healing with full-thickness dehiscence on the distal aspect of the incision,
transition plan was outlined, with anticipated local wound care until complete healing.
to work he states he now has a sitting scooter he can use so he does not have to walk around as much.
If he has any worsening symptoms,
we will likely have to keep him off work for a longer duration.

chartSnapshot.primary_reason_for_home_health_medical_n: The patient is considered homebound because he is recovering from a left partial 5th ray amputation due to gangrene. He presents with a postoperative wound dehiscence on the distal aspect of the incision, which is a full-thickness, non-healing diabetic foot wound requiring negative pressure wound therapy (wound vac) with changes three times a week. Due to this complex wound, underlying Type 2 diabetes mellitus with diabetic peripheral angiopathy, and impaired sensation in the left lower extremity, leaving the home
chartSnapshot.living_situation: The patient is recovering from a left partial 5th ray amputation due to gangrene and has a postoperative wound dehiscence on the distal aspect of the incision, which is a full-thickness, non-healing diabetic foot wound requiring negative pressure wound therapy (wound vac) with changes three times a week.
chartSnapshot.pain_assessment_narrative: The patient is recovering from a left partial 5th ray amputation due to gangrene and has a postoperative wound dehiscence on the distal aspect of the incision, which is a full-thickness, non-healing diabetic foot wound requiring negative pressure wound therapy (wound vac) with changes three times a week.
